facts.text: Parishville was named after financier David Parish, who bought the land in 1810, and the town was officially organized on March 18, 1814.
In the early 19th century, Parishville boasted 14 mills powered by the Gorge along the St. Regis River, making it an industrial hub.
David Parish, a German‑born banker, acquired roughly 200,000 acres of wilderness in 1808, laying the groundwork for the town’s development.
The West Branch of the St. Regis River runs through the community, joining the main river near Winthrop.
A dam in the center of Parishville drops the Regis River 130 feet in less than a mile, creating a dramatic waterfall at Allen Falls Reservoir.
Parishville sits in the northern foothills of the Adirondack Park, offering lush forests, vibrant fall colors, and snowy winter landscapes.
